description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe featuring a long, cylindrical metallic handle with a slightly weathered, steel-like appearance. The axe head is ornate, crafted from a bronze or brass-colored metal, and features a broad, crescent-shaped blade with a decorative bird-shaped motif on the top. The blade is single-edged, with a curved cutting edge and a pointed tip on the opposite end, suggesting both slashing and piercing capabilities. The handle appears robust and straight, designed for two-handed use, and is attached securely to the axe head. The overall design is both functional and decorative, with visible patina and wear that add to its historical character. This axe would be a striking object if found in an urban park, museum, or themed event.
description: A decorative, medieval-style battle axe with a long metallic handle and an ornate, crescent-shaped bronze blade featuring a bird motif.
description_view_0: The axe is shown in profile, highlighting the full length of the handle and the detailed, crescent-shaped blade with the bird motif.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed head-on, showing the narrow edge of the blade and the top of the handle, with the bird motif visible in silhouette.
description_view_2: The opposite profile view, again displaying the full handle and ornate blade, with the bird motif facing the other direction.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from above, emphasizing the straightness and cylindrical shape of the handle and the thinness of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, cylindrical metallic handle with a smooth surface and no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad crescent-shaped blade with a curved cutting edge, pointed tip, and a decorative bird-shaped motif on top.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted over the handle and secured.
